description: A standardized framework for mapping linguistic features across seven levels of analysis, extended by domain-specific and entity-based layers.

core_hierarchy.level_1_graphology: Smallest distinctive units of writing (punctuation, symbols, case, digital markers).
core_hierarchy.level_2_morphology: Smallest units of meaning (roots, affixes, compound structures).
core_hierarchy.level_3_lexical: Individual vocabulary items and parts of speech classes.
core_hierarchy.level_4_syntax: Structural rules governing phrases, clauses, and sentence types.
core_hierarchy.level_5_semantics: Literal meaning and core propositions (locative, causal, volitional).
core_hierarchy.level_6_pragmatics: Contextual intent and social speech acts (greetings, affirmations, social acts).
core_hierarchy.level_7_discourse: Sentence relationships and conversation flow (transitions, markers, hedging).

extension_domains.system: Operating system artifacts, logs, configurations, and tool behaviors.
extension_domains.informal_slang: Casual, dialect-specific, and modern social expressions.
extension_domains.intents: Granular communicative goals (Speech Act Theory: Informative, Directive, etc.)

extraction_entities.system_signals: Hardware and environmental telemetry (temp, load, paths, datetime).

contextual_layers.politeness: Social distancing and face-saving markers (hedging, honorifics).
contextual_layers.dialect: Regional and social variations of linguistic units.
